1.gl: to make a loud sound (like English 'hum'); by implication, to be in great commotion or tumult, to rage, war, moan, clamor
1.kj: clamorous, concourse, cry aloud, be disquieted, loud, mourn, be moved, make a noise, rage, roar, sound, be troubled, make in tumult, tumultuous, be in an uproar.

1.gl: to stray (causatively, mislead), usually (figuratively) to mistake, especially (morally) to transgress; by extension (through the idea of intoxication) to reel, (figuratively) be enraptured
1.kj: (cause to) go astray, deceive, err, be ravished, sin through ignorance, (let, make to) wander.

1.gl: properly, to make mouths at, i.e. to scoff; hence (from the effort to pronounce a foreign language) to interpret, or (generally) intercede
1.kj: ambassador, have in derision, interpreter, make a mock, mocker, scorn(-er, -ful), teacher.

1.gl: to be wise (in mind, word or act)
1.kj: [idiom] exceeding, teach wisdom, be (make self, shew self) wise, deal (never so) wisely, make wiser.

1.dv: from an unused root meaning to effervesce;

1.gl: an intoxicant, i.e. intensely alcoholic liquor

2.gl: to cross over; used very widely of any transition (literal or figurative; transitive, intransitive, intensive, causative); specifically, to cover (in copulation)

2.gl: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)

2.gl: properly, to miss; hence (figuratively and generally) to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, (causatively) lead astray, condemn

fright; concrete, an idol (as a bugbear)
2.kj: dread, fear, horror, idol, terrible, terror.
2.dv: or (shortened) אֵמָה; from the same as H366 (אָיֹם);

3.kj: also, another, any (man), a certain, [phrase] champion, consent, each, every (one), fellow, (foot-, husband-) man, (good-, great, mighty) man, he, high (degree), him (that is), hus

3.gl: properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness

3.dv: from an unused root (meaning to be perverse);

3.kj: cease, sit still, loss of time.

the little man of the eye; the pupil or ball; hence, the middle (of night)
